---
title: "01_simulation"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(mvtnorm)
library(RAS)
library(future.apply)
plan(multisession) 
```

```{r config}
n_snps    <- 300
n_disc    <- 1000 # discovery cohort (method A) (scale down for speed)
n_targ    <- 1000 # target cohort (scaled down for speed)
rho       <- 0.8 # AR(1) correlation for LD
# adaptive sliding window
skip1           <- 10 # scan every 10th SNP for speed
skip2           <- 3 # test window sizes in increments of 3
min_window_size <- 3
max_window_size <- 30
# quality control
prune_r2        <- 0.2 # LD pruning threshold
# ridge_lambda removed, breaks N(0,1) null distribution
causal_snps  <- c(150, 151, 152) # set 150, 151, 152 as the causal SNPs
effect_size  <- 0.5
# p-value thresholds
slope_thresh   <- 1e-3
davies_thresh  <- 1e-3 # confirm it's not random noise
# reproducibility
base_seed      <- 35
```

```{r ld-reference}
# Autoregressive AR(1) matrix with rho = 0.8
# simulates the LD matrix
R_true <- outer(1:n_snps, 1:n_snps, function(i, j) rho^abs(i - j))
# generate n fake patients based on the LD data
sim_genotypes <- function(n, R) {
  X <- rmvnorm(n, sigma = R)
  X <- round(X) # DNA isn't continuous
  pmin(pmax(X, 0), 2) # 0, 1, 2 mutated alleles
}
# run the random simulation
set.seed(base_seed)
X_reference <- sim_genotypes(10000, R_true)
R_empirical <- cor(X_reference) # get the empirical LD matrix to use later instead of the real one
# greedy algorithm to discard redundant SNPs
prune_ld <- function(R, thresh) {
  keep <- rep(TRUE, nrow(R))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(R) - 1)) {
    if (!keep[i]) next
    for (j in (i + 1):nrow(R)) {
      if (keep[j] && R[i, j]^2 > thresh) keep[j] <- FALSE
    }
  }
  keep
}
# apply LD pruning
pruned_mask <- prune_ld(R_empirical, prune_r2)
cat("Surviving SNPs after LD pruning:", sum(pruned_mask), "of", n_snps, "\n")
```

```{r core-functions}
# runs linear regression for every SNP to measure correlation with phenotype y
get_marginal_stats <- function(X, y) {
  # Vectorized OLS for massive speedup
  m <- ncol(X)
  yc <- y - mean(y)
  Xc <- sweep(X, 2, colMeans(X), "-")
  sxx <- colSums(Xc^2)
  sxy <- as.numeric(crossprod(Xc, yc))
  beta <- sxy / sxx
  syy <- sum(yc^2)
  rss <- pmax(syy - beta * sxy, 0)
  se <- sqrt((rss / (length(y) - 2)) / sxx)
  z <- beta / se
  z[!is.finite(z)] <- 0
  list(beta = beta, z = z)
}
# t_burden from problem statement
t_burden <- function(w, Z, R) {
  denom <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(w) %*% R %*% w))
  if (!is.finite(denom) || denom <= 0) return(0)
  as.numeric((t(w) %*% Z) / denom)
}
# scans across chromosome, testing windows at every pivotal SNIP and ouptput RAS
screen_forward_max_region_ss <- function(b_disc, z_targ, R, mask) {
  N <- length(b_disc)
  this.seq <- seq(1, N, by = skip1)
  sub.seq  <- c(0, seq(min_window_size, max_window_size, by = skip2))
  if (sub.seq[length(sub.seq)] != max_window_size) sub.seq <- c(sub.seq, max_window_size)
  y_profile <- sapply(this.seq, function(site) {
    best_p <- 1
    for (ws in sub.seq) {
      idx <- if (ws == 0) site else max(1, site - ws):min(N, site + ws)
      idx <- idx[mask[idx]]
      if (length(idx) < 1) next
      w_sub <- b_disc[idx]
      z_sub <- z_targ[idx]
      R_sub <- R[idx, idx, drop = FALSE]
      num <- sum(w_sub * z_sub)
      denom <- sqrt(as.numeric(w_sub %*% R_sub %*% w_sub))
      if (!is.finite(denom) || denom <= 0) next
      
      t_val <- num / denom
      best_p <- min(best_p, 2 * pnorm(-abs(t_val)))
    }
    -log10(max(best_p, .Machine$double.xmin))
  })
  list(x = this.seq, y = y_profile)
}
# NOTE: original ras_scan() averages -log10(p) over num_rep=5 resampled 50/50
# splits. No summary-stat analog exists (Method A has fixed discovery/
# target summary stats, nothing to resplit). This runs single-pass instead
run_one_replicate <- function(true_beta, seed) {
  set.seed(seed)
  X_d <- sim_genotypes(n_disc, R_true)
  X_t <- sim_genotypes(n_targ, R_true)
  y_d <- X_d %*% true_beta + rnorm(n_disc, 0, 3)
  y_t <- X_t %*% true_beta + rnorm(n_targ, 0, 3)
  disc <- get_marginal_stats(X_d, y_d)
  targ <- get_marginal_stats(X_t, y_t)
  scan <- screen_forward_max_region_ss(disc$beta, targ$z, R_empirical, pruned_mask)
  list(scan = scan, X_t = X_t, y_t = y_t, b_disc = disc$beta)
}
# define null hypothesis and actual effect size vector
null_beta   <- rep(0, n_snps)
signal_beta <- rep(0, n_snps)
signal_beta[causal_snps] <- effect_size
```

```{r detect}
# slope_check_window_size = 5 (the package's typical default) seemed to
# only give give only ~17-20% first-pass acceptance on true signal, because the local
# slope re-check window is inside the flat top of the causal plateau.
# scw = 8 fixes this, as acceptance goes to ~100%
detect <- function(scan, window_size, scw = 8) {
  stopifnot(window_size < length(scan$x)) # make sure window size is less than chromosome size
  out <- tryCatch({
    # run changepoint detection algorithm (from old package)
    cp <- ras_detect(
      x = scan$x, y = scan$y,
      window_size                    = window_size,
      slope_check_window_size        = scw,
      slope.p.values.threshold.left  = slope_thresh,
      slope.p.values.threshold.right = slope_thresh
    )
    # run Davies test to prove change points are statistically significant (also from old package)
    val <- ras_validate(
      this.result = cp, x = scan$x, y = scan$y,
      this.start = 1, this.skip = skip1,
      second_window_size = 15, min_signal = 2.5,
      p.value.threshold = davies_thresh
    )
    # returns locations of validated peaks (tau_hats)
    list(val = val, raw_candidates = cp$all.changepoints, error = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    list(val = list(tau_hats = numeric(0)), raw_candidates = NULL, error = TRUE)
  })
  out
}
```

## Window-size sweep for CPD: Type I error & power

```{r sweep}
n_sweep <- 20 # 20 trials for each window size
window_candidates <- c(6, 8, 10, 12, 15, 20)
sweep_results <- data.frame(window_size = window_candidates,
                             type1 = NA_real_, power = NA_real_,
                             power_center_16 = NA_real_,
                             power_edge_19   = NA_real_,
                             null_errors = NA_integer_, power_errors = NA_integer_)
# test each candidate
for (k in seq_along(window_candidates)) {
  ws <- window_candidates[k]
  # cat(sprintf("\n--- window_size = %d (scw = 8) ---\n", ws))
  null_hits <- logical(n_sweep); null_err <- 0
  # pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_sweep, style = 3)
  # Type I error test
  null_res <- future_lapply(seq_len(n_sweep), function(s) {
    # this is null hypothesis, so any gene found here is Type I error
    scan <- run_one_replicate(null_beta, seed = 10000 * ws + s)$scan
    d <- detect(scan, ws, scw = 8)
    c(hit = length(d$val$tau_hats) > 0, err = d$error)
  })
  null_hits <- sapply(null_res, function(x) x["hit"])
  null_err <- sum(sapply(null_res, function(x) x["err"]))
  # close(pb)
  hit_center   <- logical(n_sweep) # target zone
  hit_edge     <- logical(n_sweep) # track gene signal too late
  power_err    <- 0
  # pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_sweep, style = 3)
  power_res <- future_lapply(seq_len(n_sweep), function(s) {
    scan <- run_one_replicate(signal_beta, seed = 20000 * ws + s)$scan
    d <- detect(scan, ws, scw = 8)
    tau <- d$val$tau_hats
    list(
      center = any(tau >= 130 & tau <= 170),
      edge = any(tau >= 185 & tau <= 195) && !any(tau >= 130 & tau <= 170),
      err = d$error
    )
  })
  hit_center <- sapply(power_res, function(x) x$center)
  hit_edge <- sapply(power_res, function(x) x$edge)
  power_err <- sum(sapply(power_res, function(x) x$err))
  # close(pb)
  # save results
  sweep_results$type1[k]           <- mean(null_hits)
  sweep_results$power[k]           <- mean(hit_center)
  sweep_results$power_center_16[k] <- mean(hit_center)
  sweep_results$power_edge_19[k]   <- mean(hit_edge)
  sweep_results$null_errors[k]     <- null_err
  sweep_results$power_errors[k]    <- power_err
}
print(sweep_results)
```

```{r choose-window}
# choose the window size with the maximum detection power and lowest Type I Error rate
chosen_window_size <- window_candidates[which.max(sweep_results$power - sweep_results$type1)]
cat("Chosen window_size:", chosen_window_size, "(scw = 8)\n")
print(sweep_results[sweep_results$window_size == chosen_window_size, ])
```

## Full run at the chosen window size

```{r full-null}
# Type I Error test by running all null-hypothesis simulations
n_null_sims <- 100
null_res <- future_lapply(seq_len(n_null_sims), function(s) {
  scan <- run_one_replicate(null_beta, seed = 100000 + s)$scan
  length(detect(scan, chosen_window_size, scw = 8)$val$tau_hats) > 0
})
null_hits <- unlist(null_res)
cat(sprintf("\nEmpirical Type I error over %d reps: %.3f\n", n_null_sims, mean(null_hits)))
```

```{r full-power}
# Statistical power test by running signal_beta (patients w/ phenotype at SNPs 150-152)
n_power_sims <- 50
power_res <- future_lapply(seq_len(n_power_sims), function(s) {
  scan <- run_one_replicate(signal_beta, seed = 200000 + s)$scan
  tau  <- detect(scan, chosen_window_size, scw = 8)$val$tau_hats
  c(center = any(tau >= 130 & tau <= 170), 
    edge = any(tau >= 185 & tau <= 195) && !any(tau >= 130 & tau <= 170))
})
hit_center <- sapply(power_res, function(x) x["center"])
hit_edge <- sapply(power_res, function(x) x["edge"])
cat(sprintf("\nEmpirical power (centered detections) over %d reps: %.3f\n", n_power_sims, mean(hit_center)))
cat(sprintf("Edge-only detections (landed on trailing edge, not center): %.3f\n", mean(hit_edge)))
```

## Results table/figures

```{r results-table}
results <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Type I error", "Power"),
  Value  = c(mean(null_hits), mean(hit_center)),
  Reps   = c(n_null_sims, n_power_sims)
)
knitr::kable(results, digits = 3, caption = "Empirical simulation results")
```

```{r sweep-plot}
plot(sweep_results$window_size, sweep_results$type1, type = "b", col = "red",
     ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = "window_size", ylab = "Rate",
     main = "Type I error vs. power across window_size (scw = 8)", pch = 16)
lines(sweep_results$window_size, sweep_results$power, type = "b", col = "blue", pch = 16)
abline(h = 0.05, lty = 3, col = "gray50")
legend("left", legend = c("Type I error", "Power", "nominal 0.05"),
       col = c("red", "blue", "gray50"), lty = c(1, 1, 3), pch = c(16, 16, NA))
```

```{r illustration}
# run individual-level algorithm (old) for comparison
indiv_baseline_profile <- function(scan, X_t, y_t, b_disc) {
  sub.seq <- c(0, seq(min_window_size, max_window_size, by = skip2))
  if (sub.seq[length(sub.seq)] != max_window_size) sub.seq <- c(sub.seq, max_window_size)
  sapply(scan$x, function(site) {
    best_p <- 1
    for (ws in sub.seq) {
      idx <- if (ws == 0) site else max(1, site - ws):min(n_snps, site + ws)
      idx <- idx[pruned_mask[idx]]
      if (length(idx) < 1) next
      LPRS <- X_t[, idx, drop = FALSE] %*% b_disc[idx]
      fit  <- summary(lm(y_t ~ LPRS))
      p    <- if (nrow(fit$coefficients) > 1) fit$coefficients[2, 4] else 1
      best_p <- min(best_p, p)
    }
    -log10(max(best_p, .Machine$double.xmin))
  })
}
# draw visualization for comparison
rep_data <- run_one_replicate(signal_beta, seed = 300000)
scan_ss  <- rep_data$scan
scan_indiv_y <- indiv_baseline_profile(scan_ss, rep_data$X_t, rep_data$y_t, rep_data$b_disc)
final <- detect(scan_ss, chosen_window_size, scw = 8)
plot(scan_ss$x, scan_ss$y, type = "l", col = "blue", lwd = 2,
     xlab = "SNP Index", ylab = "-log10(p)",
     main = "Summary-Stat RAS vs. Individual-Level Baseline")
lines(scan_ss$x, scan_indiv_y, col = "darkgray", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
abline(v = causal_snps, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
if (length(final$val$tau_hats) > 0) {
  abline(v = final$val$tau_hats, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("True causal region", "Validated changepoint", "Summary-Stat Profile", "Individual Baseline"),
         col = c("red", "darkgreen", "blue", "darkgray"), lty = c(2, 1, 1, 2), lwd = 2)
} else {
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("True causal region", "Summary-Stat Profile", "Individual Baseline"),
         col = c("red", "blue", "darkgray"), lty = c(2, 1, 2), lwd = 2)
}
```
